title: Bridge Essay: Monitoring the neural activity associated with praying in Sahaja Yoga meditation.
concept: Mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, language as mantra.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Language as mantra — mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, verbal repetition.
**Phase context:** Mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, language as mantra.

**Monitoring the neural activity associated with praying in Sahaja Yoga meditation.** — BMC Neurosci (2023). DOI: 10.1186/s12868-023-00828-x. PMID: 37957605. BACKGROUND: Sahaja Yoga Meditation draws on many religious traditions and uses a variety of techniques including Christian prayer to reach a state known as thoughtless awareness, or mental silence. While there are many studies on the neural correlates of meditation, few studies have focused on the neural correlates of praying.
